vision.text: A small CLI that turns a season of public-shoreline observations of bull-kelp canopy into a simple, plain-language condition report a community group could read at a Saturday work-party.
vision.prompt: What should exist, and what does it make visible, felt, or possible?

spec.text: Single-file Python CLI: reads a JSON file of observations (date, site, canopy_percent, observer_alias), groups by site, and prints a condition assessment per site (healthy / declining / stressed) based on simple thresholds on the per-site mean and a coarse trend signal.
spec.prompt: What does the team want made real over the two days?

source_offerings.title: Boundary Bay & Lighthouse Park kelp canopy observations 2025-2026
source_offerings.cleared_text: Public-shoreline citizen observations of bull-kelp surface canopy. Each entry: date (ISO), site (string), canopy_percent (0-100), observer_alias (string). Locations are public coastal access points only.

boundaries.label: Observer real names and contact details
boundaries.marker_text: Real observer names + emails are held in the team's roster spreadsheet, not in the cleared dataset. Only aliases appear in any AI input.

build_request.notes: stdlib only; the team is fine with the whole frozen spec going to the TELUS build lane and the resulting tool being shown in the Tuesday gallery walk.

witnessed_working.description: Tuesday: show the CLI reading the cleared observation fixture and printing a per-site condition assessment.
acceptance_criteria: assigns condition label by threshold (healthy >=60, declining 30-59, stressed <30)
handles empty input and malformed JSON without a traceback

help_wanted: a mentor pair on the threshold choices (currently picked from the team's reading, not from peer-reviewed sources)

authorization.authorization_notes: Public-shoreline observation framing only. Thresholds are illustrative for the jam; no claim of ecological certification.
